name: ERP & Accounting

users.context: Owns the integrity of the general ledger; reviews and approves journal entries, oversees month-end and year-end close, signs off on trial balance and reconciliations. Works under deadline pressure during close week with many tabs open across sub-ledgers.
users.expertise: Expert in double-entry accounting, GAAP/IFRS, chart of accounts structure, and the ERP's posting logic. High tool fluency; impatient with slow screens and redundant clicks.

users.context: Enters supplier invoices, performs 3-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice), schedules payment runs. High-volume, repetitive data entry from PDFs and emails; frequently interrupted by vendor calls.
users.expertise: Intermediate; knows AP workflow and tax codes deeply but relies on defaults for GL coding. Keyboard-heavy worker who values tab order and barcode/scan input.

users.context: Raises customer invoices, applies incoming payments, manages dunning and collections, handles credit notes and disputes. Tracks aging buckets daily.
users.expertise: Intermediate; strong on cash application and reconciliation, moderate on revenue recognition rules.

users.context: Read-mostly access during audit windows; samples transactions, traces documents to source, tests controls, exports evidence. Cannot mutate data and must see an immutable trail.
users.expertise: Expert in audit assertions and SOX controls; low familiarity with this specific ERP UI, so discoverability and export fidelity matter.

users.context: Consumes dashboards and consolidated statements; approves high-value payments and budget variances above threshold. Mobile-first, glances at KPIs, drills into exceptions.
users.expertise: Expert in finance strategy, low patience for operational detail; needs summarised, exception-driven views.

users.context: Prepares VAT/GST returns, withholding tax filings, and reconciles tax ledgers to statutory reports. Period-driven deadlines tied to jurisdictions.
users.expertise: Expert in multi-jurisdiction tax rules and filing formats.

workflows.name: Month-end / period close
steps: Run and review accruals and prepayments
Reconcile bank, AP, AR, intercompany, and inventory control accounts
Lock the period and generate financial statements
workflows.frequency: monthly (with heavier quarter-end and year-end variants)

workflows.name: Purchase-to-Pay 3-way match
steps: Match invoice lines to purchase order and goods receipt

workflows.name: Bank reconciliation
steps: Create adjustment entries for bank fees/interest

workflows.name: Journal entry creation and approval
steps: Validate balanced and correct cost centres/dimensions

workflows.name: Order-to-Cash collections / dunning
steps: Apply received payments and clear invoices

risks: Posting to a closed or wrong accounting period, corrupting comparative financials
Unbalanced or mis-coded journal entry distorting the trial balance
Duplicate supplier invoice or duplicate payment due to weak match controls
Granting posting/approval rights that breach segregation of duties (same user creates and approves)
Editing a posted/submitted transaction without an audit trail, breaking immutability
Wrong tax code or jurisdiction causing under/over-remittance and penalties
Currency or exchange-rate error in multi-currency consolidation
Missed statutory filing or close deadline triggering fines
Reconciliation forced to balance with a plug entry that hides a real discrepancy
Mass/bulk edit applied to the wrong filtered set of records
environment_constraints: Open-plan office with frequent phone interruptions during data entry
Dual-monitor desk workflows with many concurrent records open
Hard external deadlines (close week, tax filing dates) creating time pressure
Frequent reference to scanned PDFs and email attachments alongside the ERP
Occasional remote/auditor access over VPN with read-only scope
domain_patterns: Master-detail (invoice header with line items / distributions)
Immutable audit trail with reversal/credit-note instead of edit
Dimensional accounting (cost center, project, department tags)
Document numbering series and sequential integrity
Drill-down from statement to source voucher
audit_compliance: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley) internal controls over financial reporting
Immutable, timestamped audit log of who-changed-what
accessibility_concerns: Numeric data-entry grids must be fully keyboard-navigable with logical tab order
Debit/credit and positive/negative states must not rely on colour (red/green) alone
Dense financial tables need screen-reader-friendly row/column headers
Right-aligned monospaced figures for column scanning and reduced transposition errors
High-contrast mode for long sessions to reduce eye strain
anti_patterns: Allowing free in-place edits of posted transactions instead of reversals
Hiding the audit trail or making it hard to export for auditors
Auto-balancing journals silently with a plug rather than flagging the imbalance
Defaulting GL/tax codes without making them visible and overridable
Confirmation-less bulk actions on filtered grids
Colour-only encoding of debits, credits, overdue status
Burying segregation-of-duties violations until after posting rather than blocking at entry
